author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
hari he!
tavāṅghri kamaladvaya, vilāsa⋅vikramamaya,
parāvara jagat vyāpiyā
sarvakṣaṇa vartamāna, bhaktakleśa⋅avasāna,
lāgi’ sadā prastuta hoiyā

jagatera sei dhana, āmi jagamadhya jana,
atoeva sama adhikāra
āmi kibā bhāgyahīna, sādhane vañcita dīna,
ki kāja jīvane āra chāra

kṛpā vinā nāhi gati, e bhaktivinoda ati,
dainya kari’ bale prabhu⋅pāya
kabe tava kṛpā pe’ye, uṭhiba sabale dhe’ye,
heriba se padayuga hāya
en_translation:
Hari! Your two lotus feet, full of joyous might, pervading the upper and lower worlds, Are always present, ever ready to end the devotees' suffering.

That treasure of the world, I am a person within the world, therefore I have equal right. How unfortunate am I, deprived and wretched in spiritual practice, what good is this worthless life then?

Without Your mercy, there is no way to progress. This Bhaktivinoda says with great humility at the feet of the Lord: When, receiving Your mercy, will I rise with full strength and rush to see those two feet?
